channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Approaches to tenancy in Postgres
item.description: By Simeon Griggs Multi-tenancy is a term used across various kinds of technical infrastructure, including application hosting, compute, databases, and more. For example, you may purchase cloud...

item.title: Building a Solana Account Explorer: Query On-Chain Data Like a Database
item.description: From Concept to Code In Part 1 of this series, I explained that everything on Solana is an account with five fields: lamports, data, owner, executable, and rent_epoch. That's the theory. But...

item.title: The Role of the Semantic Layer in Data Governance
item.description: Most organizations have a data governance policy. It lives in a Confluence page. It defines who owns what data, what terms mean, and who should have access. And almost nobody follows it, because...

item.title: PCA (Principal Component Analysis): Finding the Hidden Structure in High‑Dimensional Data
item.description: 1. The Problem It Solves Imagine you have a dataset with 100 features, for example, pixel values from 10×10 grayscale images, or 100 different sensor readings from a factory machine. Visualising...

item.title: Install Hexabot CLI and Create Your First AI Workflow
item.description: Install Hexabot CLI and Create Your First AI Workflow Project Getting started with a new automation platform should not take hours. In this tutorial, I’ll show you how to install the Hexabot CLI,...

item.title: Building Automated AWS Permission Testing Infrastructure for CI/CD
item.description: Originally published on graycloudarch.com. I deployed a permission set for our data engineers five times before it worked correctly. The first deployment: S3 reads worked, Glue Data Catalog reads...

item.title: Building a Wasm-in-Wasm Virtualizer (with JIT decrypted paged memory)
item.description: Intro WebAssembly was built for portability and raw speed. It was definitely not built for keeping secrets. To really protect your code, you have to compile it down to a custom, undocumented...

item.title: How I Built a Sales Agent That Scores Deal Health from Memory — No CRM Update Required
item.description: Sales reps talk to the same people for months. The context — budget concerns raised in week two, the competitor mentioned offhand in week five, the onboarding objection that nearly killed the deal...

item.title: Why Your AI Initiatives Fail Without a Semantic Layer
item.description: Your team builds an AI agent. It connects to your data warehouse. A product manager types "What was revenue last quarter?" and gets a number. The number is wrong. Nobody knows it's wrong until...

item.title: Giving AI Agents Eyes (Part 2): From Page Snapshots to Interaction Traces
item.description: In Part 1, we solved the representation problem: how to give an LLM a compact, semantic view of a web page using accessibility trees. That gave our AI agent the ability to answer "what is on this...

item.title: All of the updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s battle over OpenAI
item.description: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of...

item.title: Elon Musk Loses Landmark Lawsuit Against OpenAI
item.description: The nine-member panel took only two hours to return a verdict in favor of OpenAI on Monday, which the judge quickly adopted as her own final decision.

item.title: Elon Musk lost his case against Sam Altman
item.description: After around two hours of deliberation, the jury has reached a unanimous verdict in Musk v. Altman, the tech trial of the year. The group found that two claims were barred by the statute of...

item.title: What to expect from Google this week
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. When Google opens its doors tomorrow for its annual...

item.title: Elon Musk has lost his lawsuit against Sam Altman and OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk's claim that he was mistreated by his OpenAI cofounders failed after nine California jurors decided in a unanimous verdict that his lawsuits had been filed too late.

item.title: INTERPOL Operation Ramz Disrupts MENA Cybercrime Networks with 201 Arrests
item.description: INTERPOL has coordinated a first-of-its-kind cybercrime crackdown across the Middle East and North Africa (MENA) that led to 201 arrests and the identification of an additional 382 suspects. The...

item.title: The Signals That Matter – MIT Insider’s Panel
Google is rolling out its redesigned Workspace app icons
item.description: It's not just you - the Google Workspace apps are getting a new look. The redesigned app icons, leaked last month, are now rolling out widely, as we started noticing this morning. Users with the...

item.title: Sony is raising short-subscription prices for PlayStation Plus
item.description: Sony is hiking the starting price of one-month and three-month PlayStation Plus subscriptions in "select regions," blaming "ongoing market conditions." Beginning May 20th, 1-month subscriptions...

item.title: Inside Anduril and Meta’s quest to make smart glasses for warfare
item.description: The defense-tech company Anduril has shared new details about the augmented-reality headset for the military it’s prototyping with Meta, including a vision for ordering drone strikes via...

item.title: Fine-Tuning NVIDIA Cosmos Predict 2.5 with LoRA/DoRA for Robot Video Generation
Amazon Alexa Plus can now create AI-generated podcasts
item.description: Alexa Plus, Amazon's upgraded AI assistant, can now generate podcasts on "virtually any topic," according to an announcement on Monday. With the update, Amazon says you can give Alexa Plus a...

item.title: PaddleOCR 3.5: Running OCR and Document Parsing Tasks with a Transformers Backend
Philips Hue smart lights and a whole lot more are over 20 percent off
item.description: Woot is having a day-long sale on a range of tech, including a mix of new and open-box Philips Hue smart lighting. The retailer’s already-discounted prices are even cheaper today when you enter...

item.title: The Verge’s 2026 college graduation gift guide
item.description: Graduating from college is exciting, but it can also feel slightly terrifying. Along with celebrating a huge accomplishment, many grads jump right into looking for a job. Some might be getting...

item.title: Amazon’s new Alexa+ powered feature can generate podcast episodes
item.description: Amazon’s Alexa+ can now generate custom AI podcasts on demand, as the company expands its assistant into a personalized AI content platform.

item.title: Linus Torvalds says Linux security list is becoming ‘unmanageable’ due to AI bug reports
item.description: Linux founder Linus Torvalds said in his most recent state of the kernel post that "the continued flood of AI reports has basically made the security list almost entirely unmanageable, with...

item.title: Exclusive: Jonah Peretti explains why he sold BuzzFeed
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Jonah Peretti, who is, technically, the CEO of BuzzFeed — although that will be coming to an end very soon. Just days before we spoke, Jonah agreed to sell 52 percent of...

item.title: The Open Agent Leaderboard
Starlink raises prices across satellite internet plans
item.description: Starlink is raising prices across its satellite internet plans in the US - including Standby Mode - with the cheapest 100Mbps Residential plan going from $50 to $55 / month, as reported earlier by...
